regions.text: But no. Priestley said: 'When it had gone on growing for some months, I found that the air would neither put out a candle nor was it bad for a mouse.'
In other words, the green plant had not used up all the oxygen in the air. A Dutch scientist found that Priestley’s experiment would not work without light.
In the light a plant gives out oxygen and builds up sugars. In the dark it gives out carbon dioxide and water.
either, or: one or the other. neither, nor: not one and not the other. grownup: person who has grown up.
